title: Gonzalagunia parviflora
taxonomy: Gonzalagunia parviflora is classified within the family Rubiaceae. This family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants often referred to as the coffee or madder family. Gonzalagunia parviflora is further classified within the genus Gonzalagunia, which is a genus of flowering plants native to Central and South America. The genus Gonzalagunia is part of the tribe Psychotrieae, which is a tribe of flowering plants within the family Rubiaceae. Further classification of Gonzalagunia parviflora includes its species designation, indicating its unique characteristics within the genus.

intro: Gonzalagunia parviflora is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, as well as its ability to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Gonzalagunia parviflora contains bioactive constituents including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Gonzalagunia parviflora include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
